items.title: ‘Eerie parallel’: Archived stamps reveal Canada was prepared to ration gas in the 1979 oil crisis
items.summary_auto: Canada considered conserving oil with gasoline rationing stamps in 1979. While it never put the stamps into circulation, economist Peter Tertzakian said the proofs are a reminder that anything can happen in an oil shortage.

items.title: 3 credits short of a degree, he left NSCAD for a music career. The school is now honouring him
items.summary_auto: When Andrew Scott decided to chase a career in music and give up his studies, it was only going to be temporary. "I'm not going to be in a rock band when I'm 30," Scott told his mom. Thirty-five years later, he's still making music with Sloan. His former school is giving him a prestigious designation.

items.title: U.S. pushes ahead on deep-sea mining. The rest of the world isn’t so sure
items.summary_auto: Even as the race to mine the ocean floor for important minerals intensifies, a UN-body tasked with establishing regulations for the emerging industry ended a key global meeting this week without a new mining code.

items.title: Europe is eyeing France's nuclear shield. Should Canada follow?
items.summary_auto: As European allies quietly explore France's nuclear umbrella amid doubts about U.S. reliability, Canada faces an uncomfortable question: join or stay clear. While Ottawa rejects going nuclear, extending French deterrence to North America risks inflaming tensions with Washington — and raises stark questions about who would defend whom in a crisis.

items.title: On TikTok, a new group of folk musicians are taking the genre back to its political roots
items.summary_auto: Their lyrics are pointed, specific and reference big political events that happened just days ago. Protest music experts say the responsiveness to the current moment has helped these artists catch fire online but say they'll need to move their message into the real world if they want it to stick.

items.title: Trump says the war on Iran is 'militarily' won. Yet there's still no end — or endgame — in sight
items.summary_auto: Three weeks in, the joint U.S.-Israel combat operation has killed much of Iran's leadership, knocked out much of its ballistic missile capability, sunk nearly all of its navy and, according to U.S. President Donald Trump, has "militarily WON" already. And yet, the war goes on.

items.title: Will the far right's gains in France’s local elections pave the way to the presidency?
items.summary_auto: The recent elections mark another step in the National Rally’s effort to move from the fringes to the mainstream in French politics. Despite more professional campaigns, the party still struggles to shake its reputation for extremism.

items.title: Seabed mining talks show key issues remain unresolved
items.summary_auto: Even as the race to mine the ocean floor for important minerals intensifies, a UN-body tasked with establishing regulations for the emerging industry ended a key global meeting this week without a new mining code.

items.title: Nicholas Brendon of TV's Buffy the Vampire Slayer dead at 54
items.summary_auto: Actor Nicholas Brendon, who co-starred on TV's Buffy the Vampire Slayer as Xander Harris, has died, according to a statement from his family posted on social media.

items.title: Soaring gas prices hitting Canadians hard. And it could get worse
items.summary_auto: Canadians across the country are experiencing sticker shock at the pump as the Middle East war drives fuel prices ever higher, with transport companies and those who drive for a living especially hard hit.

items.title: Police got tip about 'dirty cop' helping trafficker 15 years before Winnipeg officer's arrest: warrant docs
items.summary_auto: One of the first red flags about now disgraced Winnipeg officer Elston Bostock came 15 years before he was arrested, when an informant warned police about a "dirty cop" helping a drug trafficker, search warrant documents obtained by CBC News say.

items.title: Stock markets dip for another straight week as U.S. war on Iran continues
items.summary_auto: Canadian and U.S. stock markets sank on Friday amid fears about the effects of the U.S.-Iran war on interest rates.

items.title: Friend of Sikh student killed on Alberta highway recalls deadly daytime shooting
items.summary_auto: Birinder Singh was driving down the highway, hoping to see Alberta’s Rocky Mountains for the first time, when a bullet from a passing vehicle ended his life.

items.title: No, we don't need high-protein boxed mac and cheese, experts say. But people want it
items.summary_auto: Kraft Heinz has just announced it's launching a high-protein mac and cheese called PowerMac that delivers 17 grams of protein and six grams of fibre per serving. But did we ... need this?

items.title: 2 men accused of fathering hundreds of kids banned from donating sperm in Quebec
items.summary_auto: Quebec's Superior Court has temporarily barred a man and his son from donating sperm after they were accused of fathering hundreds of children.

items.title: Alberta to close supervised consumption sites in Calgary, Lethbridge at end of June
items.summary_auto: Nearly five years after first announcing its plans to close the supervised consumption site at the Sheldon M. Chumir Health Centre, the Alberta government set a date to close the site later this year and replace it with services more focused on recovery and treatment.

items.title: Alberta’s 'Peterson law' leads lawyers' regulator to stop mandating Indigenous education course
items.summary_auto: The regulator for Alberta’s lawyers says it will no longer mandate Indigenous cultural competency training in advance of what Alberta Premier Danielle Smith calls the “Peterson law” coming into effect.

items.title: As Middle Eastern countries struggle to down Iranian drones, Ukraine sends 200 specialists to help
items.summary_auto: After Iranian Shahed drones hit an oil refinery in Kuwait, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy confirmed that Kyiv has sent more than 200 military specialists to the Middle East to help defend critical infrastructure from the same type of threat Ukraine has faced for years.

items.title: Netflix, Warner Music sign multi-year deal to produce artist documentaries
items.summary_auto: Netflix ​and Warner Music Group have signed an exclusive multi-year deal to produce documentary series and films exploring the lives, music and legacies of the label's storied artist ‌roster.
